ayahArabic: وَيْلٌ لِّلْمُطَفِّفِينَ
ayahEnglish: Woe to those who give less [than due],

targetTranslation: those who (referring to the cheaters in measuring mentioned later)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.

targetTranslation: when (introducing a condition in this ayah about people who take full measure for themselves)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.

targetTranslation: they took measurements for themselves (from others)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ
ayahEnglish: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.

targetTranslation: they measured out for them (refers to giving less in measure to customers)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَو وَّزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.

ayahArabic: وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَو وَّزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.

targetTranslation: they make (others) lose (when measuring for them)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَو وَّزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.

ayahArabic: أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ
ayahEnglish: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

ayahArabic: أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ
ayahEnglish: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

targetTranslation: those people over there (subject of يَظُنُّ)
ayahArabic: أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ
ayahEnglish: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

targetTranslation: those who are DEFINITELY sent forth/resurrected
ayahArabic: أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبْعُوثُونَ
ayahEnglish: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

targetTranslation: mighty/magnificent (describing يَوْمٍ, in a possessive/connected state)
ayahArabic: لِيَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: For a tremendous Day -

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلنَّاسُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلنَّاسُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?

ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتٰبَ ٱلْفُجَّارِ لَفِى سِجِّينٍ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.

targetTranslation: a deep prison (being described in كِتٰبَ الفُجَّارِ)
ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتٰبَ ٱلْفُجَّارِ لَفِى سِجِّينٍ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.

targetTranslation: made you know (وَمَآ before it makes this a question: 'and what made you know?')
ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا سِجِّينٌ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا سِجِّينٌ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا سِجِّينٌ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?

ayahArabic: كِتٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ
ayahEnglish: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.

ayahArabic: كِتٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ
ayahEnglish: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.

targetTranslation: woe/destruction! (threatening those who deny in يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ)
ayahArabic: وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: Woe, that Day, to the deniers,

targetTranslation: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
ayahArabic: وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: Woe, that Day, to the deniers,

ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahEnglish: Who deny the Day of Recompense.

targetTranslation: they strongly deny/reject (referring to those who deny the Day of Judgment)
ayahArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahEnglish: Who deny the Day of Recompense.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.

ayahArabic: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.

targetTranslation: when (referring to something that will happen in the future, followed by تُتْلَىٰ/are recited)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

targetTranslation: it is being recited (to عَلَيْهِ - to him)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

targetTranslation: our signs, our verses (that are being recited)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: كَلَّا بَلْ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

ayahArabic: كَلَّا بَلْ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

targetTranslation: what (they) (connects to كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ - they used to earn)
ayahArabic: كَلَّا بَلْ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

ayahArabic: كَلَّا بَلْ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

targetTranslation: they are earning/gaining (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
ayahArabic: كَلَّا بَلْ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّهُمْ عَن رَّبِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّمَحْجُوبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.

targetTranslation: on that day (when they will be blocked from their Lord)
ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّهُمْ عَن رَّبِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّمَحْجُوبُونَ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.

ayahArabic: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."

targetTranslation: who/which/that (referring to 'this' that you were told about)
ayahArabic: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."

ayahArabic: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."

ayahArabic: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."

ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتٰبَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ لَفِى عِلِّيِّينَ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in ʿilliyyūn.

ayahArabic: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتٰبَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ لَفِى عِلِّيِّينَ
ayahEnglish: No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in ʿilliyyūn.

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is ʿilliyyūn?

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is ʿilliyyūn?

targetTranslation: the highest ones (referring to the record books of the righteous)
ayahArabic: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ
ayahEnglish: And what can make you know what is ʿilliyyūn?

ayahArabic: كِتٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ
ayahEnglish: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed

ayahArabic: كِتٰبٌ مَّرْقُومٌ
ayahEnglish: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed

targetTranslation: he witnesses it (the record book that المقربون witness)
ayahArabic: يَشْهَدُهُ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Which is witnessed by those brought near [to Allāh].

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلْأَبْرَارَ لَفِى نَعِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure

targetTranslation: they look/watch (referring to the blessed people on thrones mentioned in عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ)
ayahArabic: عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: On adorned couches, observing.

ayahArabic: تَعْرِفُ فِى وُجُوهِهِمْ نَضْرَةَ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.

ayahArabic: تَعْرِفُ فِى وُجُوهِهِمْ نَضْرَةَ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.

targetTranslation: radiance/brightness (that you see on وُجُوهِهِمْ their faces)
ayahArabic: تَعْرِفُ فِى وُجُوهِهِمْ نَضْرَةَ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.

ayahArabic: يُسْقَوْنَ مِن رَّحِيقٍ مَّخْتُومٍ
ayahEnglish: They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.

targetTranslation: of pure wine (object of يُسْقَوْنَ - they are given to drink)
ayahArabic: يُسْقَوْنَ مِن رَّحِيقٍ مَّخْتُومٍ
ayahEnglish: They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.

ayahArabic: يُسْقَوْنَ مِن رَّحِيقٍ مَّخْتُومٍ
ayahEnglish: They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.

ayahArabic: خِتٰمُهُۥ مِسْكٌ وَفِى ذٰلِكَ فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلْمُتَنٰفِسُونَ
ayahEnglish: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.

ayahArabic: خِتٰمُهُۥ مِسْكٌ وَفِى ذٰلِكَ فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلْمُتَنٰفِسُونَ
ayahEnglish: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.

ayahArabic: خِتٰمُهُۥ مِسْكٌ وَفِى ذٰلِكَ فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلْمُتَنٰفِسُونَ
ayahEnglish: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.

ayahArabic: وَمِزَاجُهُۥ مِن تَسْنِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And its mixture is of Tasneem,

targetTranslation: a spring (object of يَشْرَبُ - drinks from)
ayahArabic: عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: A spring from which those near [to Allāh] drink.

ayahArabic: عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ
ayahEnglish: A spring from which those near [to Allāh] drink.

targetTranslation: those who (referring to the criminals mentioned in أَجْرَمُوا۟)
ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

targetTranslation: they believed (subject of يَضْحَكُونَ - they laugh)
ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

ayahArabic: وَإِذَا مَرُّوا۟ بِهِمْ يَتَغَامَزُونَ
ayahEnglish: And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.

ayahArabic: وَإِذَا مَرُّوا۟ بِهِمْ يَتَغَامَزُونَ
ayahEnglish: And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.

targetTranslation: they turned themselves/returned (to أَهْلِهِمُ their families)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.

targetTranslation: their family (referring to those who returned in انقَلَبُوٓا۟)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.

targetTranslation: they turned themselves back (to أَهْلِهِمُ their families)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.

targetTranslation: happy ones (describing a group of men)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ
ayahEnglish: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.

targetTranslation: they saw them (the believers who they mock in this ayah)
ayahArabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوْهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَضَآلُّونَ
ayahEnglish: And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."

ayahArabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوْهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَضَآلُّونَ
ayahEnglish: And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."

targetTranslation: they were sent (subject of the sentence)
ayahArabic: وَمَآ أُرْسِلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ حٰفِظِينَ
ayahEnglish: But they had not been sent as guardians over them.

ayahArabic: وَمَآ أُرْسِلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ حٰفِظِينَ
ayahEnglish: But they had not been sent as guardians over them.

ayahArabic: فَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,

ayahArabic: فَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,

targetTranslation: they are laughing (referring to the disbelievers mentioned as ٱلْكُفَّارِ in the ayah)
ayahArabic: فَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ
ayahEnglish: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,

targetTranslation: they are looking (referring to the disbelievers reclining on couches mentioned in عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ)
ayahArabic: عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: On adorned couches, observing.

ayahArabic: هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?

targetTranslation: what (referring to يَفْعَلُونَ - they used to do)
ayahArabic: هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?

ayahArabic: هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?

targetTranslation: they do/are doing (referring to the disbelievers mentioned as الْكُفَّارُ)
ayahArabic: هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?
